page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-288-lssd-metal is a bare-metal server instance configured with 288 vCPUs and 1080 GB of DDR5 memory. The system is powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 144 physical cores operating at 4.2 GHz, featuring a 504 MB L3 cache. It includes 16,242 GB of local NVMe SSD storage distributed across six drives, and does not feature any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results show top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and data decompression. The server achieved top-tier status in stress-ng multi-core testing, PassMark CPU scores, and Redis SET operations, which reached 401.21 million operations per second. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 22,887.6 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 428.24 GB/sec.

Despite the massive core count, certain multi-core benchmarks like Geekbench multi-core and image processing fell into the average tier. Additionally, while prompt processing for a medium 7B LLM model achieved top-tier speeds, text generation for a small 135M model was poor, landing in the bottom 10%. This indicates that while raw compute density and memory bandwidth are exceptionally high, specific parallel execution workloads may not scale linearly across all 288 vCPUs.

This bare-metal instance is designed for high-throughput database operations, large-scale software compilation, and static web serving, where its 161.59 GB/sec web serving throughput and top-tier database operations performance can be fully utilized. It is also suitable for memory-intensive data processing and cryptographic workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-288-lssd-metal is a bare-metal server featuring 288 vCPUs, 1080 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 16,242 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz, this instance delivers top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, database operations, and memory bandwidth. In testing, it achieved 401.21 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 161.59 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. However, tradeoffs exist, such as average Geekbench multi-core scaling and poor text generation speeds for small LLM models. This server is optimized for high-throughput database systems, intensive software compilation, and large-scale data processing workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-288-lssd-metal is a bare-metal server with 288 vCPUs, 1080 GB DDR5 RAM, and 16,242 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and multi-core CPU performance.

tagline: A bare-metal platform combining 288 Intel Xeon vCPUs with 16 TB of local NVMe storage for high-throughput database and compute workloads.

16,242 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across six drives
Top-tier multi-core CPU, database operations, and memory bandwidth performance
Tradeoffs include average Geekbench multi-core scaling and poor small-model LLM text generation
Suited for high-throughput databases, software compilation, and static web serving
